overallSummary: Fabio Giglietto maintains a targeted and active social media presence across Mastodon, LinkedIn, and Bluesky, accumulating a total of 3,580 followers. His content primarily revolves around computational social science, disinformation research, and academic opportunities. While LinkedIn boasts the largest follower count and strong engagement for professional news, Bluesky stands out with the highest average engagement rate per post, particularly for new research announcements. Overall, Fabio effectively uses different platforms to communicate varied aspects of his academic and professional work.

contentThemes.description: Updates on research projects, methodologies, paper publications, and discussions related to disinformation, coordinated behavior, and information operations.
Announcements for grants, hiring for postdoctoral researchers, and research opportunities for PhD students.
Updates on new project releases (e.g., CooRnet v2.0) and upcoming presentations at conferences.

recommendations: Prioritize Bluesky for major research announcements (e.g., new papers, project releases) given its consistently high engagement rate for such content.

Continue leveraging LinkedIn for professional announcements like grant awards and hiring calls, as this platform demonstrates strong performance for these types of updates due to its large professional audience.
Consider cross-posting key research milestones across all platforms, tailoring the caption slightly to suit each platform's audience and maximize overall reach.

Explore creating more discussion-provoking content on Mastodon, similar to the post about methodological approaches, as this platform seems conducive to deeper academic exchanges despite a smaller audience.

platformInsights.bluesky: Bluesky exhibits the highest average engagement rate, proving particularly effective for announcing new publications and highlighting upcoming presentations. Its audience appears highly engaged with direct research updates and academic news.
platformInsights.mastodon: Mastodon provides a focused academic sphere, effectively used for sharing specific project updates (like CooRnet v2.0) and facilitating discussions on research methodologies. It caters to a smaller, more niche community but shows consistent interest in detailed research work.
platformInsights.linkedin: LinkedIn is the platform with the largest follower base and demonstrates strong performance for formal professional announcements, such as securing new grants and recruitment drives, indicating its value for professional networking and talent acquisition.

topPerformingContent.content: Just published our new paper on coordinated inauthentic behavior on social media platforms!
New research opportunity for PhD students interested in computational social science. Reply for details!
Excited to announce that our research team has been awarded a new grant to study information operations across social media platforms.
Our team has just released v2.0 of CooRnet, with improved algorithms for detecting coordinated link sharing behavior.
